meta.title: California AI Impact Gap Map
meta.subtitle: Three case studies showing where AI investment breaks before it reaches Californians

gapTypes.meaning: The chain exists but we don't know if it works
Evidence is strong but investment hasn't followed
gapTypes.policyAction: Direct state procurement and grant funding

gapTypes.meaning: The intervention is early — outcomes take years to materialize
gapTypes.policyAction: Protect long-horizon funding from budget cycles

gapTypes.meaning: Both the research and the investment are missing
No one is tracking whether AI is helping or hurting
gapTypes.policyAction: Mandate impact reporting from AI companies operating in CA

examples.title: Entertainment Economy

breakdownCallout.text: California's annual film and TV tax credit — with no requirement to measure AI's net impact on employment

structuralProblem.text: The 2023 WGA/SAG-AFTRA strike lasted 118 days. AI replacement was the central issue. No state data system tracked what happened to workers afterward.

examples.policyAction: Require studios receiving CA tax credits to report AI usage and its employment impact annually

harmChain.name: AI replaces writers, artists, voice actors
No tracking. No mitigation. No data.

examples.title: Wildfire Detection

breakdownCallout.text: The LA wildfires killed 29 people and destroyed 18,000 structures. AI-powered detection systems existed. Warning dissemination to high-risk communities did not.

whatDoesntExist.text: A funded system to ensure AI wildfire alerts actually reach mobile-home parks, non-English speakers, and elderly residents in evacuation zones.

examples.policyAction: Fund last-mile alert dissemination infrastructure targeting underserved communities at wildfire risk

examples.title: California's Economy

breakdownCallout.text: Amount California spends annually measuring the net economic impact of AI on workers, wages, and communities

examples.policyAction: Commission a biennial California AI Economic Impact Report, tracking wages, employment, and inequality by region and demographic

chain.name: AI drives productivity gains in CA industry
Living standards preserved for all Californians

deliverables: 3 fully-mapped causal chains for CA priority domains
Interactive opportunity map showing CA-relevant gaps
Policy action cards with cost estimates for each gap
